citeinfo.title: Assessment of water-quality in the California Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta during a North Delta directed flow action: August - October 2018 (ver. 2.0, September 2021)
Assessing spatial variability of nutrients, phytoplankton and related water-quality constituents in the California Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta at the landscape scale: High-resolution mapping surveys

descript.abstract: This data release documents the spatial and temporal variability of nutrients and related water quality parameters at high spatial resolution in the North Delta in the Sacramento-San Joaquin River Delta of California, USA. The data set includes nitrate, ammonium, phosphate, dissolved organic carbon, temperature, conductivity, dissolved oxygen, turbidity, and chlorophyll . Data-collection cruises were conducted under three different environmental/flow conditions in August, September, and October 2018 that coincided with conditions prior to, during, and following a directed flow action or pulse of agricultural return water organized by the California Department of Water Resources and California Natural Resources Agency .
descript.purpose: The objective of this project is to document the variability of nutrients and related water quality parameters at high spatial resolution in the North Delta (California, USA). This data release reports measurements that include nitrate, ammonium, phosphate, dissolved organic carbon, temperature, conductivity, dissolved oxygen, turbidity, and chlorophyll.
descript.supplinf: See files: Delta Water Quality Mapping 2018 Data Corrections and Offsets.xlsx; Delta Water Quality Mapping 2018 Data Dictionary.xlsx for further information regarding the datasets.

idinfo.useconst: Although this data set has been used by the U.S. Geological Survey, U.S. Department of the Interior, no warranty expressed or implied is made by the U.S. Geological Survey as to the accuracy of the data and related materials. The act of distribution shall not constitute any such warranty, and no responsibility is assumed by the U.S. Geological Survey in the use of this data, software, or related materials. Any use of trade, product, or firm names is for descriptive purposes only and does not imply endorsement by the U.S. Government.
idinfo.native: Environment of Metadata Creation: Microsoft Windows 10 Enterprise Version 1803 (OS Build 17134.885)

Method 445.0 - In Vitro Determination of Chlorophyll a and Pheophytin a in Marine and Freshwater Algae by Fluorescence

Microbial colonization on Natural and Artificial Macrophytes in a Phosphorus-limited, Hardwater Lake
Automated Separation and Conductimetric Determination of Ammonia and Dissolved Carbon Dioxide
Continuous Flow Reduction of Nitrate to Ammonia with Granular Zinc
Seeing the Light: The Effects of Particles, Dissolved Materials, and Temperature on In Situ Measurements of DOM Fluorescence in Rivers and Streams
Using Continuous Underway Isotope Measurements To Map Water Residence Time in Hydrodynamically Complex Tidal Environments
Methods of Analysis by the U.S. Geological Survey National Water Quality Laboratory; Determination of Inorganic and Organic Constituents in Water and Fluvial Sediments
Optical Properties of Dissolved Organic Matter (DOM): Effects of Biological and Photolytic Degradation
Procedures for Using the Horiba Scientific Aqualog® fluorometer to Measure Absorbance and Fluorescence from Dissolved Organic Matter
Biovolume Calculation for Pelagic and Benthic Microalgae
Phytoplankton Pigments in Oceanography: Guideline to Modern Methods

citeinfo.title: A temperature Compensation Method for CDOM Fluorescence Sensors in Freshwater
An Improved Phycobilin Extraction Method

attracc.attraccr: EXO sondes were calibrated before use in the field with the KorEXO software. For calibration, the sonde sensors are submerged one at a time in standards, then the verified chemical concentration is recorded within the instrument. If the instrument measures an unknown value of the parameter, it refers to the calibration information to calculate the concentration of the chemical in the water (EXO User Manual, pg. 81).

dataqual.logic: Redundancy is built into the system, meaning many of the parameters included in the data set are measured by 2 different instruments. Data was displayed in real-time for on the spot user verification. Topology rules were applied to points, lines, and polygons to ensure the integrity of the geospatial data.
dataqual.complete: Data set is considered complete for the information presented, as described in the abstract. Users are advised to read the rest of the metadata record carefully for additional details.

horizpa.horizpar: We conducted a visual check that the GPS data we recorded matched the boat GPS. Data was also displayed in real-time, allowing us to spot check GPS data underway. In addition, GPS location information was compared to field notes that described known locations and features.

procstep.procdesc: High-resolution water quality mapping surveys of the Sacramento-San Joaquin River Delta (California, USA) were conducted four times in 2018 during the months of August, September, and October using the USGS R/V Mary Landsteiner, a 26-foot (~8 meter) long motorboat. For each map, transects were conducted near slack tide on August 23, September 05, September 13, and October 04. Each day covered similar reaches of the North Delta (~50 km). Periods of interest in the data sets include August 23, 2018 (8:49 - 12:00; 14:20 - 16:07), September 05 (7:40 - 11:00; 13:00 - 15:30), September 13 (13:30 - 15:40) and October 4 (8:45 - 10:30).

All data directly logged to the flow-through data collection system was processed using pandas software library in Python to remove periods of compromised data (e.g. flow blockages, bubbles), to apply instrument corrections and unit conversions, and to apply a centered 20 second median filter to the time series. Final nitrate values were obtained by regressing instrument response against nitrate concentrations obtained from laboratory measurements of discrete samples collected over three of the mapping days.

enttyp.enttypd: Comma Separated Value (CSV) file containing data.

overview.eaover: The data dictionary is titled Delta Water Quality Mapping 2018 Data Dictionary_North Delta. This file lists and describes the labels in tables.
overview.eadetcit: The files are available on the landing page:https://www.sciencebase.gov/catalog/item/5e7ceb88e4b01d5092751c18

Any use of trade, product, or firm names is for descriptive purposes only and does not imply endorsement by the U.S. Government.
